entry.title: Let's Create a JSON Path Query Tool In SAP UI5 As Simple as Possible
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

Most of us are aware about JSON and its significance in SAP UI5 for all the data handling, often its observed that we need a complex coding to manipulate the Json Data, so we are trying to address this by Query based program originally introduced by Stefan Goessner's original post and we will crate a tool around it.  

A frequently emphasized advantage of XML is the availability of plenty tools to analyze, transform and selectively extract data out of XML documents. XPath is one of these powerful tools.

It's time to wonder, if there is a need for something like XPath4JSON and what are the problems it can solve.

  • Data may be interactively found and extracted out of JSON structures on the client without special scripting.
  • JSON data requested by the client can be reduced to the relevant parts on the server, such minimizing the bandwidth usage of the server response.

If we agree, that a tool for picking parts out of a JSON structure at hand does make sense, some questions come up. How should it do its job? How do JSONPath expressions look like?

We will be using a library developed by  Stefan Goessner's original post introducing JSONPath in 2007.

We will be developing a UI5 App using this library so that we can us the app for doing a query over a JSON object.

Skil Set Involved: - JSON, UI5, Java Script and Any SAP web Based tool (WEB IDE, BAS)

For a Quick Reference we can check below details

 

 

 

 

We will be developing a UI5 App using this library so that we can us the app for doing a query over a JSON object.

Skill Set Involved: - JSON, UI5, Java Script and Any SAP web Based tool (WEB IDE, BAS)

Once Application is developed, the application should look like below.

Step1 :- Create a SAP UI5 Basic app by using template below structure

 

 

Step2: - we will be taking library reference from below URL, and I have modified as per my need.

https://code.google.com/archive/p/jsonpath/downloads

Seep3:- we will create a new folder ‘external’ and add  a js file name ‘jsonpath.js’ and the content will be like below.

 

 

/* JSONPath 0.8.0 - XPath for JSON
 *
 * Copyright (c) 2007 Stefan Goessner (goessner.net)
 * Licensed under the MIT (MIT-LICENSE.txt) licence.
 */

sap.ui.define([
	"sap/m/library"
], function (mobileLibrary) {
	"use strict";
	// shortcut for sap.m.URLHelper
	var URLHelper = mobileLibrary.URLHelper;

	return {

		jsonQuery: function (obj, expr, arg) {
			var P = {
				resultType: arg && arg.resultType || "VALUE",
				result: [],
				normalize: function (expr) {
					var subx = [];
					return expr.replace(/[\['](\??\(.*?\))[\]']/g, function ($0, $1) {
							return "[#" + (subx.push($1) - 1) + "]";
						})
						.replace(/'?\.'?|\['?/g, ";")
						.replace(/;;;|;;/g, ";..;")
						.replace(/;$|'?\]|'$/g, "")
						.replace(/#([0-9]+)/g, function ($0, $1) {
							return subx[$1];
						});
				},
				asPath: function (path) {
					var x = path.split(";"),
						p = "$";
					for (var i = 1, n = x.length; i < n; i++)
						p += /^[0-9*]+$/.test(x[i]) ? ("[" + x[i] + "]") : ("['" + x[i] + "']");
					return p;
				},
				store: function (p, v) {
					if (p) P.result[P.result.length] = P.resultType == "PATH" ? P.asPath(p) : v;
					return !!p;
				},
				trace: function (expr, val, path) {
					if (expr) {
						var x = expr.split(";"),
							loc = x.shift();
						x = x.join(";");
						if (val && val.hasOwnProperty(loc))
							P.trace(x, val[loc], path + ";" + loc);
						else if (loc === "*")
							P.walk(loc, x, val, path, function (m, l, x, v, p) {
								P.trace(m + ";" + x, v, p);
							});
						else if (loc === "..") {
							P.trace(x, val, path);
							P.walk(loc, x, val, path, function (m, l, x, v, p) {
								typeof v[m] === "object" && P.trace("..;" + x, v[m], p + ";" + m);
							});
						} else if (/,/.test(loc)) { // [name1,name2,...]
							for (var s = loc.split(/'?,'?/), i = 0, n = s.length; i < n; i++)
								P.trace(s[i] + ";" + x, val, path);
						} else if (/^\(.*?\)$/.test(loc)) // [(expr)]
							P.trace(P.eval(loc, val, path.substr(path.lastIndexOf(";") + 1)) + ";" + x, val, path);
						else if (/^\?\(.*?\)$/.test(loc)) // [?(expr)]
							P.walk(loc, x, val, path, function (m, l, x, v, p) {
							if (P.eval(l.replace(/^\?\((.*?)\)$/, "$1"), v[m], m)) P.trace(m + ";" + x, v, p);
						});
						else if (/^(-?[0-9]*):(-?[0-9]*):?([0-9]*)$/.test(loc)) // [start:end:step]  phyton slice syntax
							P.slice(loc, x, val, path);
					} else
						P.store(path, val);
				},
				walk: function (loc, expr, val, path, f) {
					if (val instanceof Array) {
						for (var i = 0, n = val.length; i < n; i++)
							if (i in val)
								f(i, loc, expr, val, path);
					} else if (typeof val === "object") {
						for (var m in val)
							if (val.hasOwnProperty(m))
								f(m, loc, expr, val, path);
					}
				},
				slice: function (loc, expr, val, path) {
					if (val instanceof Array) {
						var len = val.length,
							start = 0,
							end = len,
							step = 1;
						loc.replace(/^(-?[0-9]*):(-?[0-9]*):?(-?[0-9]*)$/g, function ($0, $1, $2, $3) {
							start = parseInt($1 || start);
							end = parseInt($2 || end);
							step = parseInt($3 || step);
						});
						start = (start < 0) ? Math.max(0, start + len) : Math.min(len, start);
						end = (end < 0) ? Math.max(0, end + len) : Math.min(len, end);
						for (var i = start; i < end; i += step)
							P.trace(i + ";" + expr, val, path);
					}
				},
				eval: function (x, _v, _vname) {
					try {
						return $ && _v && eval(x.replace(/@/g, "_v"));
					} catch (e) {
						throw new SyntaxError("jsonPath: " + e.message + ": " + x.replace(/@/g, "_v").replace(/\^/g, "_a"));
					}
				}
			};

			var $ = obj;
			if (expr && obj && (P.resultType == "VALUE" || P.resultType == "PATH")) {
				P.trace(P.normalize(expr).replace(/^\$;/, ""), obj, "$");
				return P.result.length ? P.result : false;
			}
		}
	};

});

 

 

Step4 :- Modify the View1.xml Content for the required screen.

 

 

<mvc:View controllerName="Test.TEST.controller.View1" xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" displayBlock="true" xmlns="sap.m"
	xmlns:code="sap.ui.codeeditor">
	<Shell id="shell">
		<App id="app">
			<pages>
				<Page id="page" title="JSON Path Query Tool">
					<content>
						<OverflowToolbar id="otb1">
							<Button type="Accept" text="Execute" press="onExecutePress"/>
							<Button type="Accept" text="Template" press="onTemplatePress"/>
						</OverflowToolbar>
						<!--<Button text="ok" press=".onPress"></Button>-->
						<Input id="inputText" placeholder="Enter Query" class="sapUiSmallMarginBottom"/>
						<code:CodeEditor id="inputTextCode" type="json" value='' height="200px"/>
						<TextArea id="idQueryOutput" editable="false" growingMaxLines="7" width="100%" height="50%"></TextArea>
					</content>
				</Page>
			</pages>
		</App>
	</Shell>
</mvc:View>

 

 

 

 

Step5:- View1.controller.js

 

 

sap.ui.define([
	"sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller",
	"../external/jsonpath",
	"sap/ui/model/json/JSONModel"
], function (Controller, jsonpath, JSONModel) {
	"use strict";

	return Controller.extend("Test.TEST.controller.View1", {
		onInit: function () {

		},
		onExecutePress: function () {

			var codeEditorData = JSON.parse(this.getView().byId("inputTextCode").getValue());
			this.getView().setModel(new JSONModel(codeEditorData), "codeEditorDataModel");
			var queryString = this.getView().byId("inputText").getValue().trim();
			var productNames = jsonpath.jsonQuery(codeEditorData, queryString);
			this.getView().byId("idQueryOutput").setValue(productNames);

		},
		onTemplatePress: function () {
			var jsonData = {
				"store": {
					"book": [{
						"category": "reference",
						"author": "Nigel Rees",
						"title": "Sayings of the Century",
						"price": 8.95
					}, {
						"category": "fiction",
						"author": "Evelyn Waugh",
						"title": "Sword of Honour",
						"price": 12.99
					}, {
						"category": "fiction",
						"author": "Herman Melville",
						"title": "Moby Dick",
						"isbn": "0-553-21311-3",
						"price": 8.99
					}, {
						"category": "fiction",
						"author": "J. R. R. Tolkien",
						"title": "The Lord of the Rings",
						"isbn": "0-395-19395-8",
						"price": 22.99
					}],
					"bicycle": {
						"color": "red",
						"price": 19.95
					}
				}
			};

			this.getView().byId("inputTextCode").setValue(JSON.stringify(jsonData));
			this.getView().byId("inputText").setValue("$.store.book[*].author");
		}
	});
});

 

 

Regards,

Dibyajyoti Nanda

https://www.linkedin.com/in/dibyajyoti-nanda-23907429/

entry.title: Re: Sales Order - Level Approval Workflow and release from Credit Block 2 in 1
entry.content:
Dears,

In sales order output i have also configured EDI output ( Outbound IDOC) to third party system.

How i can control that EDI output should only Trigger, After Final Workflow Approval. ???

Means EDI output should not trigger unless Sales order Completely approved.

entry.title: How to split the single delivery into multiple invoices.
entry.content:
Is it possible to split the single outbound delivery invoice request into multiple invoices. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to split the single delivery into multiple invoices.
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

It is not possible to split a single outbound delivery into multiple invoices. But if you would like to set up additional parameters for the invoice split you can do this in the fine tuning activity Customer Invoice Split Rule in your Business Configuration

Best regards,

Arjun T H

SAP Product Support

entry.title: Onetime Deduction Uploads
entry.content:
Hello all,

Need to ask you all advise on specific usecase

We have a process where by we have to upload onetime deduction by Non-HR user more than one location on regular basis.

Landscape : successfactors with a Backend ECC system, payroll on ECC and is processed on Payroll Control center.

We always had a minimal uploads to do so we sought LSMW however with this new deduction which encompasses multiple Non HR user it means multiple upload files which is not efficient.

I have seen Fiori lets users upload document which use a service to upload the file similarly do we have any SAP delivered service which we can resuse to upload IT 0015 to ECC?

My idea was if the Non HR users upload the file from Fiori , hopefully service will then point/send this data to CPI which then converts data in to IDOC's hoping this Idoc will be sent to ECC.

Let me know if you have any better way.

Many thanks

Ajay

entry.title: Re: Get data from oData binding
entry.content:
you can use this snippet to get the bound data for the whole table.
if you're using sap.m.Table, change the aggregation binding "rows" with "items". it should work also.

oTable.getBinding("rows").getAllCurrentContexts().map(row => { return row.getObject() } )

entry.title: Re: Project Stock and Closing of WBS
entry.content:
Hi Neeren,

to answer your question straight, when you have open quantity of project stock in the respective plant/storage location. you would not be able to close the WBS or the respective project.

whenever you create a demand for project stock and procure project stock against that Project/WBS, then the costs of that project stock gets posted on balance sheet G/L's which would be commitment cost on that project. So it is like balances exists on that project. you would first need to clear out those open postings by returning the unused project stock to unrestricted stock via stock transfer.

Once you do the stock transfer, you should be able to close the WBS/Project.

Regards,

Sai

entry.title: Does AIF support batch OData configuration? (API_BUSINESS_PARTNER)
entry.content:
Does AIF support batch OData configurations? We are currently using the API_BUSINESS_PARTNER, and our middleware team is sending a batch POST call (comprising multiple POST requests) to create multiple Business Partners in the S/4HANA system.

Can we use AIF to validate the data before it is posted? Since AIF is generally designed to process one JSON file per call, how can it be set up to handle a batch request? Is there a way to configure AIF to support such batch processing?

entry.title: Re: Condition Contract for Sales - Manual Delta Accruals
entry.content:
Dear gvictor,

We could likely follow your suggestion during the data migration phase by creating a CC with a REBD or REJ1 condition type to "balance" the business volume with the total accruals that will be booked.

Unfortunately, this approach cannot be applied to day-to-day operations. Instead, we’ll look for a BADI or enhancement to update the REA2 amount in the final settlement document (or the RED2 in the automatic delta accruals), including the amounts posted by the RENT condition type (stored in table WCOCOF) through the manual delta accrual settlement document. Currently, if we compare the accrued amounts in WCOCOF (sum of the KWERT column) with the REA2 amount retrieved in the final settlement document, they are unbalanced, as REA2 does not account for the amounts posted by RENT.

Do you know a BADI or enhancement useful for this?

Thanks again

Michele

entry.title: Not Found Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Delivery Document in CBC
entry.content:
Hi Team,

In SAP Public Cloud, We have a requirement to assign Sales Document Type DL and Delivery Document NL. However, in a standard configuration, copy control is not assigned; even in public cloud CBC configuration, we do not find in the CBC the Define Copying Control for Sales Document to Delivery Document. Therefore we can't create the delivery. See the below screenshot for the error message. How can we assign it? 

In addition, there are no maintain  schedule line item categories in the public cloud. Only the assignment is there; however, we don't find item category NLN with schedule line category, and in the assignment, the system can't allow creating new ones. We can't understand which schedule line categories belong to the 641 movement type. 

 

Furthermore, we didn't find the Define Copying Control for Delivery Document to Billing Document; the copy control Delivery Document NL to Billing Document F8.

We need your help and suggestions to achieve these requirements. What will be the steps? Do we need to send a request to SAP to create and configure these? or any other way. Please provide your valuable feedback.

Thank You
Hasnat

entry.title: Cannot find "Saltuation" field in OData service.
entry.content:
I'm using the SAP Odata service (we have s4/hana) to create new business partners. I've found all the fields that I need to map data for except one: On the main business partner screen there is a field called "Salutation". I can't find it.

I've exported the entire OData Business Partner structure into a metadata file and the only thing I've seen with the name Salutation under the main Business Partner entity is is "BPFinancialServicesExtn". There's a field called "BPLetterSalutation".

I've done a GET call on many of the business partners that use this Salutation field and it's definately not stored in this field. In fact I've expanded every single child element inside the Business Partner Odata service for these business partners and the value we store in this SALUTATION field isn't available in ANY of the data. Does anyone know where this is mapped to?

entry.title: Food for thought: What’s your favorite holiday dish?
entry.content:
Hello everyone :)!

Happy holidays!

Cooking has always been for me a source of pleasure, joy and pride. As a child, I was inspired by a special cooking book for children that I got as a prize. Cooking was a way for me to be creative and experiment. I really enjoyed it. Later, when I grew-up, it became more of a necessity. But then, the Corona came and I spent a lot of time at home. That made me invest more time in hobbies and brought me back to my old passion for cooking. I found my self cooking all sorts of new dishes such as childhood snacks and more complex dishes (you can see some examples in this post). While I was advancing in my cooking journey I noticed what impact food can have on people and especially on their feelings.

My holiday season was back in October. During that period I thought how cooking became a way for me to make the people I love happy and to show them that I care and think of them. During the holiday meals, I made special dishes to my family and friends and they also made some for me, which made me very happy:).

Below I share pictures of dishes I made recently and the background story that spiced them:). 

I would love to see pictures you've taken of your day-to-day cooking/holiday dishes and to hear the stories behind them in the replies below:).

Please be sure to follow this discussion to be updated when I and others add new pictures and stories and maybe even secret recipes and useful techniques 🙂

I wish you a great holiday and a happy new year :).

Moshe

Let's start:

Family holiday celebration - finally all of us came together:) 

In October we had our holiday season which included the new year’s celebration. For the first time in many years, the extended family was able to celebrate together. I was very moved by the event and decided to take the crazy challenge and cook for everyone and invite them over to my house :).

It wasn’t easy to cook for eleven people, including six children 🙂 but everyone loved it and almost I didn’t have to use the dishwasher as the dishes were mostly emptied ;).

Here is a zoom-in on the special dish :):

Flat black pot with spicy fish and potatoes

The power of meatballs!

We all know how children can be picky about food. Well, my youngest Peleg is :). I tried different dishes and methods, but he mostly kept eating Pasta Bolognese and schnitzels. He always kept speaking of the amazing meatballs he loved in the kindergarten when he was 5 years old (he's 7.5 years old). After few attempts to make them on my own, I thought why not contact the cook who made the food in that kindergarten and ask for recipe. So, I did, and he gladly shared the recipe and the results were astonishing 😁:

Peleg ate them every day for at least 2 weeks. It became a hit as another mother asked for recipe after I shared with her a sample for her kid:).

I have a habit to cook once in two months all sorts of dishes, so the kids will have a variety of dishes to choose from and this dish became one of the regulars in the menu:).

This is my daughter’s favorite and she shared that girls in class always ask for a bite:

Plastic box with two pockets, one for meatballs with red sauce and white rice.

Cooking together

Cooking is also a mean for bonding and enrichment. On weekends, we now have a habit of cooking together. I let the kids take different roles in the cooking process: preparing the ingredients and vegetables, inventing recipes, designing and eating 🙂 :

And Segev invented a recipe for fried Cheder balls:

They also helped me make these Falafel balls (Peleg loved them as well)

Thanks a lot for reading so far. As mentioned in the beginning, please share your cooking and food pictures in the replies below and stories behind them.

Please continue to follow this discussion as I will upload new stories and pictures from time to time.

Happy holidays!

entry.title: Re: How do I read text from BP Role Customer from S/4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello @mpredolim 

Unfortunately it is not possible to call function modules from CDS views

it is not possible to read SAPscript texts from a CDS view. The reason is that the content of the texts is stored in a special packed format in the database which can only be unpacked by ABAP command IMPORT FROM DATABASE. Therefore, reading the content of the texts is only possible using ABAP. The database itself is not able to do this.

 

Please submit a feature request if you would like the functionality provided in a future release by 

following the KBA 2963059 ( https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2963059 )

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: How to create new condition tables for batch determination?
entry.content:
Hi,

I would like to know how to create new condition tables for batch determination, and if it's possible to customize the selectable key fields.
Best regards.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create new condition tables for batch determin...
entry.content:
Hi,

The condition records can be created/changed in the following activities/SSCUI:

Batch Determination for Inventory Management

  • Create Condition Tables to Determine Batches for Inventory Management
  • Change Condition Tables to Determine Batches for Inventory Management

Batch Determination for Production

  • Create Condition Tables to Determine Batches for Production
  • Change Condition Tables to Determine Batches for Production

Batch Determination for Sales and Distribution

  • Create Condition Tables to Determine Batches for Sales and Distribution
  • Change Condition Tables to Determine Batches for Sales and Distribution

Please note that the standard Condition Tables provided by SAP cannot be modified. However, you have the option to create or change your own Condition Tables.

When creating or changing the condition tables, you can customize them using the key fields provided by SAP. It is not allowed to create new key fields.

You can refer to the KBA 3267138 - Condition records and Batch determination in Public Cloud, for a list of available key fields.

Thanks and regards.

entry.title: Error Handling in the deletion of Debit Memo Request created via RRB process[Transaction DP92]
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore the error-handling process involved during deletion of Debit Memo Requests (DMRs) that are created using transaction DP92. In case you need more information on how DMR’s are created via DP92 or on setting up of Advance Execution Service Order please refer this blog.

With the release of SAP S/4HANA OP 2023 FPS3, SAP has introduced support for the deletion of DMRs created via DP92.  

  • When an Advanced Execution Item is added to a Service Order, the ‘Transfer to Billing’ status is initially set to ‘Pending.’  
  • Upon creating a DMR through DP92, this status is updated to ‘Successful.’  
  • When a DMR / CMR is deleted, there is a determination carried out to find out the service order which is referenced in the DMR and using this info following actions are taken on service order document  
  • Deletion of Header-to-Header Document Flow  
  • Deletion of Item-to-Item document flow  
  • Reset of “Transfer to Billing “status [if applicable] 

Event Handling During DMR Deletion 

DMR/CMR Deletion operation are handled by listening to standard RAP events raised by SD Module. 

  1. Deletion of the DMR: 
  • The deletion of a DMR triggers the RAP event R_DEBITMEMOREQUESTTP~DELETED. 
  • A handler is implemented for this event, responsible for resetting the Transfer to Billing status at the Service Order item level. 
  • The AIF interface SV_DMR_HDR under the namespace /CMSRV is used to monitor this handler. 

 

  1. Deletion of DMR Line Items: 
  • Deleting individual line items of a DMR triggers the RAP event R_DEBITMEMOREQUESTTP~ITEMDELETED. 
  • A handler for this event manages the deletion of transaction history at the line-item level. 
  • The AIF interface SV_DMR_ITM under the namespace /CMSRV is used to monitor this handler. 

System Requirements for Monitoring Errors 

To effectively monitor errors related to the above events, ensure the following prerequisites: 

  • The AIF interfaces SV_DMR_ITM and SV_DMR_HDR must be available under the /CMSRV namespace. 
  • RAP utilizes BGPF to execute these events. All settings related to BGPF must be activated. 

Error Log Monitoring 

The transaction /AIF/ERR can be used to view the error logs of the handlers mentioned above. 

  • Enter the required parameters and press F8. 

 

 

  • The subsequent screen will display all messages matching your criteria. 

 

 

Message Status Icons 

The message screen includes icons that represent different statuses. Refer to the legend on the screen for details. 

 

 

Resolving Locking Errors 

In case of errors during the update process such as transaction locking, such messages are logged for reprocessing, Users can select the affected messages and reprocess them.

entry.title: Create WBS from Excel via Enterprise Project API with OAuth Authentication
entry.content:
Overview:

With the SAP Fiori app “Migrate Your Data” (F3473) , you can migrate enterprise projects including their WBS elements to an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition system. However, it’s not supported to migrate WBS elements of existing projects. 

This document explains how to create WBS elements in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems for existing enterprise projects from an excel file via a Python script. The Python script uses the Enterprise Project API (oData) with OAuth Authentication.

Key Concept

OAuth is an open standard for authorization that allows third-party applications to access protected resources on behalf of a resource owner. It enables users to grant third-party applications access to their resources stored on another service provider without exposing their credentials.

Assuming you have a central Identity Provider and want to use a Python client to access the Enterprise Project API to create WBS elements, the process is as follows:

 

Communication Management

Set up Communication Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to enable Python to call the Enterprise Project API.

Communication system:

 

 Communication arrangement

After saving, note down the Client ID, Token Service URL,  Authorization URL (from OAuth 2.0 Details) and API URL used in the client configuration.

 Config.json:

{   
    "API_URL":              "https://<your host>-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_ENTERPRISE_PROJECT_SRV;v=0002", 
    "Token_Service":        "https://<your host>-api.s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/public/bc/sec/oauth2/token", 
    "Authorization_URL":    "https://<your host>s4hana.ondemand.com/sap/bc/sec/oauth2/authorize", 
    "OAuth_ClientId":        "EPPM_TEST_OAUTH",
    "Excel_File":            "EnterpriseProjectSample.xlsx"
}

 

 

 

Consume API with OAuth Authentication via Python

Load configuration:

#load config
try:
    with open('config.json', 'r') as file:
        config_data = json.load(file)
        client_id=config_data['OAuth_ClientId']
        client_secret=''
        authorization_base_url=config_data['Authorization_URL']
        token_url=config_data['Token_Service']
        api_url = f"{config_data['API_URL']}/"
        excel_file = config_data['Excel_File']

except FileNotFoundError:
    # config not found
    print("config file not found,please config first")
    exit

Create OAuth2 session:

# Create OAuth2 session
def create_oauth_session(client_id:None,pkce:None,redirect_uri:None,token=None, state=None, token_updater=None):

    oauth = OAuth2Session(
        client_id,
        token=token,
        scope=None,
        pkce = pkce,
        redirect_uri=redirect_uri,
        token_updater=token_updater
    )
    return oauth

oauth = create_oauth_session(client_id,pkce='S256',redirect_uri=redirect_uri)

Request Authorization certification:

@app.route('/login')
def login():
    authorization_url, state = oauth.authorization_url(authorization_base_url)
    session['oauth_state'] = state
    print(authorization_url)
    return redirect(authorization_url)

Grant Authorization

You can login with http://localhost:5000/login.

You will be redirected to the Identity Provider.

 

Access Token

An access token is generated if the permission is granted successfully.

@app.route('/callback')
def callback():
    token = oauth.fetch_token(token_url, client_secret=client_secret,
        authorization_response=request.url,include_client_id=True,verify=False)
    session['token'] = token
    print('token is:"{token}"')
    ex_str = "Authenticated! Start testing Enterprise Project API. \nToken:" + str(token)

Request to create WBS:

  createWBS_url = 'http://127.0.0.1:5000/createWBS'
  return redirect(createWBS_url)

# Create WBS with authorized token
@app.route('/createWBS')
def protected_resource():
    response=creatingWBSfromExcel.create_wbs(api_url=api_url, oauth=oauth, excel_file=excel_file )
    return response

Create WBS Elements from Excel via Enterprise Project API

Prepare data and upload from Excel

The WBS elements in the Excel should look like this:

ProjectID

WBS

ParentWBS

Description

DEMO1205Y1

DEMO1205Y1wp1

 

Project Initialization

DEMO1205Y1

DEMO1205Y1wp2

 

Requirements Gathering

DEMO1205Y1

DEMO1205Y1wp3

 

System Design

DEMO1205Y1

DEMO1205Y1wp11

DEMO1205Y1wp1

Stakeholder Identification

DEMO1205Y1

DEMO1205Y1wp12

DEMO1205Y1wp1

Project Charter Development

DEMO1205Y1

DEMO1205Y1wp21

DEMO1205Y1wp2

Current State Assessment

DEMO1205Y1

DEMO1205Y1wp211

DEMO1205Y1wp21

Future State Visioning

DEMO1205Y2

DEMO1205Y2wp1

 

Market Research

The WBS elements are created line by line in a separate transaction. Therefore, the parent WBS element should be on top of the list.

    # Load the Excel file
    try:
        df = pd.read_excel(excel_file)
    except Exception as e:
        log += f"Failed to read Excel file: {str(e)}\n"
        return log

Fetch CSRF token:

    headers = {"X-CSRF-TOKEN": "Fetch"}

    # Fetch CSRF token
    response = oauth.get(f"{api_url}A_EnterpriseProject?$top=1", headers=headers, verify=False)
    response.raise_for_status()
    csrf_token = response.headers.get('x-csrf-token')

    log = f"Start test and get csrf_token: {csrf_token}\n"

Retrieve Project information:

    # Retrieve project UUID
    filter_str = f"Project eq '{project_id}'"
    response = oauth.get(f"{api_url}A_EnterpriseProject?$filter={filter_str}", headers=headers, verify=False)
    response.raise_for_status()
    response_json = xmltodict.parse(response.text)['feed']['entry']
    project_uuid = response_json['content']['m:properties']['d:ProjectUUID']

Convert WBS data from Excel to Json:

    project_element = {
        "ProjectElement": f"{wbs}",
        "ProjectElementDescription": f"{row.get('Description', '')}",
        "ProjectUUID": f"{project_uuid}",
        "PlannedStartDate": f"{row.get('Start Date', '')}",
        "PlannedEndDate": f"{row.get('End Date', '')}",
        "ProfitCenter": f"{row.get('Profit Center', '')}",
        "ResponsibleCostCenter": f"{row.get('Cost Center', '')}",
        #"InvestmentProfile": invest_profile,
        "to_EntProjElmntBlkFunc": {
            "EntProjTimeRecgIsBlkd": bool(row.get('Blocking Indicator - Time Recording', False)),
            "EntProjStaffExpensePostgIsBlkd": bool(row.get('Blocking Indicator - Concur Expense', False)),
            "EntProjServicePostingIsBlkd": bool(row.get('Blocking Indicator - Activity Allocation', False)),
            "EntProjOtherExpensePostgIsBlkd": bool(row.get('Blocking Indicator - Other Expense Pstng', False)),
            "EntProjPurchasingIsBlkd": bool(row.get('Blocking Indicator - Purchasing', False))
        }
    }

Call API Odata Service:

    if not parent_wbs:
        url = f"{api_url}A_EnterpriseProject(guid'{project_uuid}')/to_EnterpriseProjectElement"
    else:
        parent_wbs_filter = f"ProjectElement eq '{parent_wbs.upper()}'"
        response = oauth.get(f"{api_url}A_EnterpriseProjectElement?$filter={parent_wbs_filter}", headers=headers, verify=False)
        response.raise_for_status()
        response_json = xmltodict.parse(response.text)['feed']['entry']
        parent_wbs_uuid = response_json['content']['m:properties']['d:ProjectElementUUID']
        url = f"{api_url}A_EnterpriseProjectElement(guid'{parent_wbs_uuid}')/to_SubProjElement"

    response = oauth.post(url, headers=headers, json=project_element, verify=False)

Error can be parsed by:

    error = xmltodict.parse(response.text)['error']['message']['#text']

Testing / Execution

Now you can start testing.

  1. Create an enterprise project via Project Control – Enterprise Projects (Fiori ID F3215).
  2. Prepare the WBS elements in an Excel file.
  3. Run Python (for example: OAuthMain.py).
  4. Open your browser and login to http://localhost:5000/login
  5. Grant authorization.
  6. The creation of the WBS elements will be triggered automatically.
  7. You can also trigger WBS creation via http://localhost:5000/createWBS, in case the session is still valid.

You can generate an executable file to share with your teammates in case they don’t have Python installed.

pyinstaller --onefile your_script.py

 

Possible Errors:

  • Excel file is still in editing. In this case, you need close the Excel file and call http://localhost:5000/createWBS.
  • The WBS elements have already been created.

Failed to create Project Element DEMO1205Y2WP2 due to Project Element DEMO1205Y2WP2 existes

entry.title: Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
entry.content:
In this blog, we will explore how SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management can process electronic invoices received by email.

As of January 2025, a new legal mandate in Germany requires businesses to be able to receive invoices electronically from their suppliers. For more information about the law in general, check out this blog New B2B electronic invoicing law in Germany: What you need to know.

Multiple solutions available

While this blog focuses on handling electronic invoices received by email, it's important to note that there are other solutions available. SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management also supports electronic invoices received through the Peppol network. There are additional SAP offerings for handling electronic invoices received from your supplier. To get a complete list, check out the Supported Business Scenarios for Germany.

For more information on some of these alternatives, refer to the following blogs: 

  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices by Email in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or SAP ERP
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol network in in SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or SAP ERP

Overview of the Process

As a company in Germany, you have two options to manage electronic invoices received by email with SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

Option 1: Automatic extraction from the email and upload of the XML using SAP Build Process Automation.

  1. File Extraction from Email and Upload:
    You use the Supplier Invoice File Extraction from Email and Upload to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management task automation to automate the extraction and upload of supplier invoices, which you receive as an email attachment, to your SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. See Supplier Invoice File Extraction from Email and Upload to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management (6JX).
    As a result, the system automatically creates a draft invoice for each uploaded file, attaching the file to the draft invoice.
  2. Validation and Mapping
    The business system forwards the electronic invoice to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.  The system validates the invoice and maps it to the standard format used in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. As a result, if the invoice is complete and valid, its status changes to "Parked and Completed."
  3. Verifying and Posting Invoice
    If manual adjustments are needed, such as correcting missing or incorrect fields, you can edit the invoice using the Manage Supplier Invoices Centrally app    . Then you submit the invoice for posting. This will trigger an approval workflow.
  4. Further Processing
    An invoice for the received electronic document is created in your connected business system, and you can further process it.

Option 2: Manual extraction from the email and manual upload of the XML file in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

  1. Extracting the XML File
    You received an email with an attached electronic invoice file, and you extract the XML file to your local system.
  2. Uploading the File
    You upload the XML file to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management using the Upload Supplier Invoices Centrally app. For more information, see Upload Supplier Invoices Centrally. As a result, the system automatically creates a draft invoice for each uploaded file, attaching the file to the draft invoice.
  3. Validation and Mapping
    The system forwards the electronic invoice to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition.  The cloud edition functionalities validate the invoice and map it to the standard format used in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. As a result, if the invoice is complete and valid, its status changes to "Parked and Completed."
  4. Verifying and Posting Invoice 
    If manual adjustments are needed, such as correcting missing or incorrect fields, you can edit the invoice using the Manage Supplier Invoices Centrally app. Then you submit the invoice for posting. This will trigger an approval workflow.
  5. Further Processing
    An invoice for the received electronic document is created in your connected business system, and you can further process it.

How to get started?

To be able to use the scenarios you need to do the following configurations in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management:

  1. Set up the connection from SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition. Configuration steps described on SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. See Setting Up Connectivity.
  2. Activate data extraction of the XML file with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition in the Manage Processing Services app. See Activate Data Extraction of XML Electronic Invoice with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, Cloud Edition.

To complete the integration, you must do some configurations in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition:

  1. Set up the connection from SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition to SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management. See Setting Up Connectivity.
  2. Specify client credentials required to enable your business system to communicate with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition, using OAuth 2.0 client authentication. See Creating Service Bindings Using OAuth 2.0 Client Authentication for Invoices Received by E-Mail (Germany Only)

    Note:     If you have already integrated SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition to receive invoices through the Peppol network, you only need to create the additional service binding and use the other setting as they are already configured.

For information about accepting and processing electronic invoices received through the Peppol network, please refer to Receiving Electronic Invoices Through the Peppol Network in SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management.

By leveraging SAP Ariba Central Invoice Management, businesses in Germany can seamlessly transition to accepting electronic invoices, ensuring compliance with the new legal requirements while optimizing their invoicing processes.

entry.title: BAS - Load error in app using Attachment Services Reuse Component RAP V4 OData Service
entry.content:
Hello Expert,

I have created a Fiori element application based on RAP V4 OData service and my application is working fine and getting data on list page and object page.

We have created the RAP V4 service and created the application based on this. Now I want to add the "Reusable Attachment component" in my object page. And added the configuration same as provided in SAPUI5 demokit for ODATA V4 version. 

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/d869d7ab3caa48b2a20dc20dfa248380

entry.title: Posting attachments to ECC from SAP BTP Cloud Foundry workflow [Build process automation]
entry.content:
Hi All,

We had a requirement to post attachments with different extensions to backend [ECC]. Required functionality was to fetch file content for respective posted invoice from azure, which can be one or multiple files with different extensions, convert it in acceptable ECC format and post it to ECC.

When I was implementing this functionality, I couldn’t find any straightforward blog which would help me explain how to send attachments from workflow to ECC or any other backend system. Hence, I explored some other options, made it successful and thought I should share so that it can be useful and can be referred by others who want to achieve similar kind of functionality in their projects. Hope this helps.

We had a requirement where we wanted to send attachments to ECC. This requirement was to post attachments to backend only if invoice posting is successful.

Here our complete logic flow is as below:

  • User will raise invoice request where user has flexibility to add documents with any extension.
  • Request will be stored in azure and BTP workflow will be triggered for the same for posting the invoice to backend.
  • Once invoice posting is successful, workflow will fetch the attachment file content, and as is send to ECC with required parameters against related invoice.

 

Steps to add logic flow for posting attachments to ECC.

  • First, I am checking if posting of invoice is successful, as we want to send attachments only for success scenario and not for failures

 

 

 

  • If it’s successful, fetch the files from invoice request, one invoice can have multiple files with different extensions.

 

 

 

 

  • Fetch the file content from azure by sending file name in required payload format to API call. Collect the response from azure in response variable for next step.

 

 

 

 

 

  • Prepare payload for posting document to ECC. Header parameters and payload content is what we ll require to send to entity set.

 

 

 

 

 

Header parameters as above.

And file content we will send as is from azure response variable to “Request variable” section to avoid any corruption or additional quotes which gets added due to reassignment.

 

  • Once posting step is completed, we will handle the notification for success/error. And in next step we will check if files are remaining to post.

 

  • Then, we’ll check flag [which we can set by shifting or removing files form original file array of one or multiple files] for remaining documents, as we are posting one document at a time. Once all files are posted, we will complete the workflow.

 

 

 

  • From backend we must convert the file content [which is base64] in X-string to process it further. 

Once all files are posted in backend, we can view all posted attachments against invoice as below.

 

 

Using this approach, you can post files easily to backend, as well you can view attached files from backend.

 

 

That is all folks, this is how I achieved the posting files to backend. You can modify the flow and steps as per your own requirement. Good Luck!

entry.title: DataServices Salesforce Adapter retirement - a free alternative solution within BODS
entry.content:
If you are here, you may already know the issue, but for the rest:

The problem: The current out-of-the-box way of connecting to Saleforce with DataServices won’t work after Salesforce retires its REST API v21, in the summer of '25. 

Regarding the end of Salesforce compatibility SAP is citing legal restrictions in the related SAP Note:

3004914 - SAP Data Services and Salesforce.com compatibility

Cause:

The Adapter for SFDC as a part of the Data Services using is indeed on SFDC API version 21 only. Due to
the legal restrictions, SAP applications do not connect to SFDC directly or provide direct interface into
SFDC systems. Thus SAP Data Services cannot connect directly to SFDC as well. The supported version
21 is prior to the restrictions and thus it is being provided in an ‘as is’ state and no further enhancements
will be provided.

SAP proposes two possible solutions:

  • with BODS: purchase a 3rd party adapter instead of the current one: Simba Salesforce Adapter for SAP Data Services
  • leave BODS and resolve the Salesforce integration with SAP Data Intelligence Cloud.

In this post I describe a solution which is free* and is within BODS. If you have other data sources  besides Salesforce and have a large invesment in BODS; you may prefer to stick with it. With the below solution you can do it without additional cost.

The key building blocks will be:

user defined transform + python + simple-salesforce + Salesfroce Bulk API 2.0 (+ CSV)

User defined transform (UDT)

In a nutshell, User Defined Transform is the way to extend your BODS out-of-the-box capabilities with whatever you want. Well… as long as it fits into 255 varchar columns. This is quite a limitation in 2024, so the work-around we will apply is to dump our Salesforce resultset into a local CSV. It is less elegant, but it works with longer fields as well. 

Simple-salesforce

Simple-salesforce is a Python library for... you guessed it, Salesforce. You may use the Salesforce API directly, but I found this library quite simple useful. The latest version supports Bulk API 2.0 as well. To be able to use it within BODS, we would need to import it to our environment as follows:

Adding simple_salesforce to local python:
Add to the Environment variables:

  • E:\SAP BusinessObjects\Data Services\DataQuality\python
  • E:\SAP BusinessObjects\Data Services\DataQuality\python\Scripts
  • E:\SAP BusinessObjects\Data Services\bin

Download and install pip

Documentation: https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/installation/#get-pip-py

1. Download the script from https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py

2. Command prompt: python get-pip.py

Install simple_salesforce:

python -m pip install --target="E:\SAP BusinessObjects\Data Services\DataQuality\python\Lib\site-packages" simple_salesforce

Python code of the User Defined Transform

 

from simple_salesforce import Salesforce, SalesforceLogin, SFType

# set salesforce connection details
username = 'aaa'       # !!!
password = 'bbb'       # !!!
security_token = 'ccc' # !!!
domain = 'xxx'         # !!!
sf_version = '59.0'

# login and get session
session_id, instance = SalesforceLogin(username=username,
password=password, security_token=security_token, sf_version=sf_version, domain=domain)
sf = Salesforce(instance=instance, session_id=session_id)

queryName = record.GetField(u'i_queryName')
querySOQL = record.GetField(u'i_querySOQL')

# execute query
results = sf.bulk2.Account.query(
query=querySOQL, max_records=None, column_delimiter='PIPE', line_ending='CRLF'
)

# write results to local drive as CSV -- !!!customize to your local folder!!!
for i, data in enumerate(results):
  with open(f"F:/FileDatasource/results/{queryName}-part-{i}.csv", "w", encoding="utf-8") as bos:
  bos.write(data)

o_result = queryName + ' loaded successfully'
record.SetField(u'o_result', o_result)

del queryName
del querySOQL
del o_result

 

A few notes regarding the above code:

  • Unfortunately I have experienced that BODS crashed with ntdll.dll error, after closing the Python editor when opening from the Repository\Transforms (ie modifying the main object) BUT less so when modifying an instance of the same UDT object inside a dataflow. The trace I have found in the Windows event log points to: C:\Windows\SYSTEM32\ntdll.dll
    A related SAP Note on the topic: 3485456 - Designer crashes frequently without error message -
    SAP BusinessObjects Data Services
    Eventually, my recommendation is to develop Python code outside of BODS and later import the UDT from XML or ATL.
  • Unicode Getfield crash: 1256795 - User Defined Transform with Python code is crashing - Data
    Services
  • sf.bulk2.Account.query – Here Account doesn’t seem to matter at all. I have tried with sf.bulk2.blalba.query – and it worked the same way. What seem to matter is the SOQL query. Consequently, you can solve different Salesforce table loads with the same single UDT.
  • I used PIPE as column delimiter, and CRLF as line ending for the CSV. These are not the default settings for CSV-s in BODS. Keep it in mind when defining your CSV source. I used these becuase these are less frequently used characters in Salesforce content. It did the trick for me, but keep in mind that this is still not a bulletproof solution. You may want to check for these characters in your content before dumping as CSV. 
  • Don't forget to customise the UDT to your environment:
    • username
    • password
    • token
    • domain
    • CSV local folder

Attached you can find the export of the User Defined Transform.

*If you find the above solution useful please leave a comment with your country / city. I would like to create a map with pins - hopefully - around the globe.

entry.title: How to Use Formulas in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
In SAP Build Apps, transformation formulas play a vital role in dynamically manipulating data within applications. Like Excel formulas, these predefined functions and expressions allow you to build logic seamlessly without the need for custom coding. By leveraging transformation formulas, you can efficiently transform data for UI bindings, API responses, and other app logic requirements.

Formulas serve as one of the primary component binding types in SAP Build Apps. This means they can be applied in almost any part of the application to bind and manipulate data dynamically. For instance, when binding a "Text" or "Dropdown" component value, you can simply select the binding icon in the Properties tab and choose "Formula" as the binding type. This flexibility extends to tasks such as validating data fetched from an API, validating the data, Changing the Formats of display data, String and Arithmetic functions, etc., Using formulas, you can access all existing variables in the application that can then be modified.

We developed a prototype for creating a Business Partner using SAP Build Apps integrated with SAP Build Process Automation. Here, SAP On-Prem systems are connected to read data from the Business Partner API and display it as Value Helps for dropdown values

 

 

Here’s an overview of common transformation formulas that we used in SAP Build Apps for reading the data from backend and displaying as value helps:

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Build Apps provides an extensive range of built-in functions to simplify transformations, covering categories like:

  • Text Operations: To Check if a value exists, Select Query , or match patterns using regex.
  • Date Functions: Manipulate and format dates dynamically to the local format.
  • Color and Object Handling: Dynamically update colors based on conditions, objects, and other data types.
  • Arithmetic Operations: Perform calculations and computations directly such as sum, absolute values, is_even, is_odd, divide, multiply etc,.

 

In addition to built-in formulas, you can design custom transformation formulas to handle specific functionalities within your app. Here are a few common use cases:To map an input field of Dropdown type, we can map the data that needs to read from a datasource

  • Mapping Data to a Dropdown Input Field -  To bind a dropdown component to a field in data source as a value help,we can map the desired fields as:

MAP(data.ZCDV_BusinessPartnerRole1, {label:item.BPRoleText,value:item.BusinessPartnerRole})

 

 

  • Displaying Unique Fields Without Duplicates - To ensure a dropdown contains only unique values and no duplicates with different combinations

UNIQUE_BY_KEY(MAP(data.ZCDV_BupaCountry1,{label:item.CountryText, value:item.Country}), "value")

  • Fetching State/Region Data Based on Selected Country input - To dynamically filter and display regions or states in dropdown based on the selected country

 

MAP(SELECT(data.ZCDV_BupaCountry1, item.Country == pageVars.bp_inputs.country), {label: item.RegionText, value: item.RegionName})

 

  • Checking if Input is Empty - To validate whether an input field is blank or contains a value for conditional processing

IS_EMPTY(pageVars.bp_inputs.country)

 

  • Text transformations– Converting Variables to Uppercase/Lowercase using text transformations.Also, we can validate pattern for email using IS_EMAIL or REGEX functions.

UPPERCASE(data.A_BusinessPartner1[0].Initials)

Note: In SAP Build Apps, custom logic or formulas cannot be directly written for all data sources. Only APIs that are exposed can be utilized for any kind of data transformation or manipulation.

I hope this blog post provides you with a understanding of formulas in SAP Build Apps and how they can be leveraged to enhance data manipulation within your applications.

entry.title: How to do SELECT SINGLE/SELECT UP TO 1 ROWS using CDS
entry.content:
Introduction

It is common to need one line for a specific table on SAP. For example, most part of the companies that I worked you can get the Purchase Order plant from EKPO, because it is always the same.

Unfortunately, CDS does not provide a way to do a select up to 1 rows, but here is the workaround that I've been using for a while and SAP seems to be adopted as well

Important to mention that I do my best to avoid AMDP. For all personal experience, the performance and reusability is worse than common data definition/CDS Views

Getting the first (or last) row

The trick is that, for the Purchase Order example, we do not have the item to have the right cardinality. So, we must have all the keys available to perform the select to one specific record

To do that, we can create a view that will return the first or the last (does not matter as the information must be the same anyway)

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Purchase Order First Item'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity Z_I_EKPOFirstItem
  as select from I_PurchaseOrderItem
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      cast( min(PurchaseOrderItem) as ebelp ) as FirstItem
}
group by
  PurchaseOrder

Joining to get the full data

Now, if we do a select on the Z_I_EKPOFirstItem by Purchase Order (EBELN), it will return always only one row, which is the full key of EKPO. Then, we can use this view and join with the standard view to have access to all EKPO fields and select the one that we initially needed

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Purchase Order'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity Z_I_PurchaseOrder
  as select from I_PurchaseOrder

    inner join   Z_I_EKPOFirstItem   on Z_I_EKPOFirstItem.PurchaseOrder = I_PurchaseOrder.PurchaseOrder

    inner join   I_PurchaseOrderItem on  I_PurchaseOrderItem.PurchaseOrder     = I_PurchaseOrder.PurchaseOrder
                                     and I_PurchaseOrderItem.PurchaseOrderItem = Z_I_EKPOFirstItem.FirstItem
{
  key I_PurchaseOrder.PurchaseOrder,
      I_PurchaseOrderItem.Plant,
      I_PurchaseOrder.PurchaseOrderType
}

You can see that the plant comes from I_PurchaseOrderItem, which is selected from Z_I_EKPOFirstItem using the full key.

This way, we will never have more than one record and it will not duplicate any records.

Tables with more than two key fields

You can do the same for tables with more key columns than only two, but then it gets uglier

Create the same way the CDS View to select only one record from your table, for this example, MARD - it is just an example, I am not saying that this example makes sense hehe
But now, as you have more than one key, MIN or MAX will not work properly, then you can use it concatenating all keys - do not forget to include all key columns

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'MARD First Row'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity Z_I_MARDFirstRow
  as select from mard
{
  key matnr                     as Matnr,
      min(concat(werks, lgort)) as firstRowKey
}
group by
  matnr

After that, you can use it in your view, but you will need to use a substring according to each field position and length.
For this example WERKS and LGORT have 4 as length. You can use it directly on the join like below

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'MARA'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity Z_I_TS_MARA
  as select from mara

    inner join   Z_I_MARDFirstRow on Z_I_MARDFirstRow.Matnr = mara.matnr

    inner join   mard             on  mard.matnr = Z_I_MARDFirstRow.Matnr
                                  and mard.werks = substring( Z_I_MARDFirstRow.firstRowKey, 1, 4 )
                                  and mard.lgort = substring( Z_I_MARDFirstRow.firstRowKey, 5, 4 )
{
  key mara.matnr as Matnr,
      mard.lgort,
      mard.werks
}

 

Any better ideas?

I could not think of something better than this approach, but it seems that SAP is using that as well, for example you can check the standard view I_BillingDocFirstItem

@EndUserText.label: 'Get BillingDocument Item Position'
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ISDBILLDOCFSTIT'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@ClientHandling.algorithm: #SESSION_VARIABLE
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@VDM.viewType: #COMPOSITE

@ObjectModel.usageType.serviceQuality:#D
@ObjectModel.usageType.sizeCategory: #XL
@ObjectModel.usageType.dataClass: #MIXED


define view I_BillingDocFirstItem
  as select from I_ExciseDueDocBase
{
  I_ExciseDueDocBase.BillingDocument,
  min(I_ExciseDueDocBase.BillingDocumentItem) as BillingDocumentItem
}
group by
  BillingDocument

entry.title: 深度探索 | 轻松入门SAP分析云脚本编写（四）
entry.content:
SAP 分析云提供了强大且丰富的图表样式和交互方式来满足用户对于数据可视化以及企业计划的需求。在实际应用中，我们的用户对图表的交互有时会有更定制化的需求，比如希望创建隐藏和显示图标的按钮。因此，SAP 分析云提供了脚本功能来进一步提高图表和交互的定制程度。

本系列文章将继续分享在 SAP 分析云中通过简单的脚本代码实现增强故事功能的实践经验，旨在帮助没有深厚编程背景的用户学习SAP分析云故事中的脚本编写。如果您对之前的文章感兴趣，请查看文章末尾的合集。

一、前言

本文通过 SAP分析云 的最新功能（如优化设计体验和脚本支持），从现有的故事入手，逐步添加小代码段来提升用户体验。在之前的分享中，我们介绍了如何利用书签功能和简易脚本实现故事状态的一键重置。现在，我们将介绍几个基础的脚本元素，旨在帮助你进一步提升故事的吸引力。


二、 学习资源

在深入探讨如何通过代码片段增强您的故事之前，我们将先介绍一些非常不错的资源。在learning.sap.com上有许多优质课程，例如 SAP Analytics Cloud 中的脚本编写基础免费课程，为您提供在新的优化故事环境中进行脚本编写的基础知识。

您可以在下面找到课程的链接。这是一个很不错的引导式学习的资源。

  • 获取基本脚本技能以在 SAP Analytics Cloud 中扩展故事

除了上述课程资源外，我们还想分享一些关于 SAC 环境中可用的方法和功能的信息。这些信息来自官方的帮助文档，我们特别挑选了三项关键内容，以便您能够直接查阅：

  1. 脚本（优化故事体验和分析设计器）
  2. 优化故事体验API参考指南
  3. 分析设计器API参考指南

三、动态图表更新：维度与度量的脚本控制

在本文中，我们将深入探讨如何利用脚本实现度量的快速切换，这一功能与度量输入控件的操作相似。我们将详细讨论如何通过编码API来复用统计图表，并调整其中的特定元素。

如果您已经熟悉SAP Analytics Cloud（SAC），您可能已经了解如何在故事中创建度量输入控件或维度输入控件。这些控件使用户能够通过从故事中添加的列表中选择所需项目，来替换统计图中的度量或维度。

随着优化故事的推出，这一操作并没有改变。但我认为这是一个很好的契机，来介绍如何通过脚本更改统计图的元素。此外，通过编写脚本来观察在更改“度量”或“维度”后，其他统计图元素如何响应，这将有助于您掌握类似的模式。

本示例中，我们提供了一个包含统计图的故事场景。该统计图的数据模型涵盖了3个不同的维度和3个不同的度量。通过这个实践案例，您将能够更深入地理解如何通过脚本控制这些元素，进而优化您的数据分析和故事叙述。 从而优化你的数据分析和故事讲述。

 

在本示例中，我们构建了一个包含单一度量和单一维度的故事。为了展示如何通过脚本实现度量和维度的切换，我们将引入几个按钮，使您能够直观地看到所需的操作步骤。

以下是完成后的效果图展示。左侧的统计图作为我们进行脚本编写的基础图表，而右侧的统计图则通过输入控件实现了相同的功能。通过这些控件，您可以轻松地交换图表中的项目。

正如上图所示，您可以通过点击按钮来选择图表中应显示的度量和维度。实际上，脚本的核心逻辑非常简洁明了，其步骤包括：识别图表中当前显示的度量；从统计图中移除现有的度量；将新的度量添加至统计图中。

在实际编写代码的过程中，您可以利用快捷键Ctrl + 空格来触发代码提示功能（注意切换到英文输入法）。

接下来让我们看一下第一个按钮背后的实际代码：

// 从统计图中移除当前度量并添加新度量

// 使用 getMembers() 函数创建一个变量来保存当前统计图度量

var TheCurrentMeasure = Chart_1.getMembers(Feed.ValueAxis);
console.log(TheCurrentMeasure[0]);

// 移除该度量，使统计图暂时没有度量Chart_1.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis,TheCurrentMeasure[0]);

// 向统计图添加所需度量，以替换之前删除的度量 Chart_1.addMember(Feed.ValueAxis,"[Account].[parentId].&[TR]");

 

让我们逐步剖析代码的功能。首先，我们创建了一个名为 TheCurrentMeasure 的变量，并使用 getMembers() 函数将其初始化为 Feed.ValueAxis。

简而言之，getMembers()函数的作用是返回一个数组，该数组包含了所有应用于统计图表的度量。由于我们的统计图表中仅包含一个度量，因此这个数组仅包含一个元素。您可以通过检查浏览器控制台来验证这个数组的内容，正如我在代码的第5行所做的那样，我将数组的第一个元素输出到了控制台。

我们已经成功创建了一个变量，并用图表中当前的度量值对其进行了赋值。接下来，我们需要在添加新度量之前，先移除这个现有的度量。在代码的第8行，我们调用了removeMember()函数，这个函数的作用是从指定的维度（在这个例子中是ValueAxis）中移除特定的度量。具体来说，我们移除了数组中的第一个元素。

 

Chart_1.removeMember(Feed.ValueAxis,TheCurrentMeasure[0]);

 

请注意，我们使用 TheCurrentMeasure[0] 来引用数组的第一个元素。尽管数组中可能包含多个度量，但在本例中，我们的焦点仅在于元素[0]。

现在，我们接近完成整个过程。我们已经从统计图表中移除了原有的度量，接下来的最后一步是添加一个新的度量。为此，我们调用addMember()函数来引入一个名为[TR]的新度量：

 

Chart_1.addMember(Feed.ValueAxis,"[Account].[parentId].&[TR]");

 

这个过程包括识别现有的度量、将其移除，然后引入一个新的度量来替代它。这与度量输入控件的工作原理相同，但同时也展示了通过脚本编写API所能达到的能力和灵活性。当然，我们同样可以应用类似的代码逻辑来对维度进行完全相同的操作。

 

// 创建一个变量来保存应用于统计图的当前维

var TheCurrentDimension = Chart_1.getDimensions(Feed.CategoryAxis);
console.log(TheCurrentDimension[0]);

// 从统计图中删除该维 － 它是数组元素 [0] Chart_1.removeDimension(TheCurrentDimension[0],Feed.CategoryAxis);

// 向统计图添加“渠道”维 Chart_1.addDimension("Channels",Feed.CategoryAxis);

这段代码的设计思路其实与用户更换度量的思路非常相近，但通过特定的函数调用，我们简化了代码编写的过程。

您可能已经注意到，即使在脚本编写示例中，当您更改维度或度量时，统计图的标题也会随之更改。虽然目前还没有直接通过脚本API来更改统计图标题的功能，但您仍然可以采用一些方法来动态地调整标题。

为了实现这一点，您可以向统计图标题中添加动态文本——具体来说，可以通过使用“添加”>>“动态文本”的功能，将账户和维度信息嵌入到统计图的标题中。这样，标题就能根据当前显示的维度和度量动态变化，增强了图表的交互性和信息的即时性。

 

通过适当设置格式，每当您利用脚本API更改度量或维度时，标题也会自动更新以反映这些变化。这种做法确保了统计图的标题与图表内容保持同步，从而提升了图表的可读性，并增强了信息传达的准确性。

以下是一个示例，展示了如何使用按钮来切换度量值，使得统计图和动态标题能够同时响应变化：

 

四、总结

本文主要介绍了在如何通过 SAC 的脚本API来动态管理和更新统计图表中的度量和维度。我们还讨论了如何通过添加动态文本到图表标题中，来实现标题的自动更新，以保持与图表内容的同步。希望这些内容能在您创建交互式故事或教程时提供实用的指导。

祝编码愉快！

 

原作者：@Daniel_Davis

原文链接：Start Your scripting Journey The Easy Way With SAP Analytics Cloud | Part Four

entry.title: Can Blocked Contacts be Deleted in Business ByDesign?
entry.content:
Is there a way to delete all blocked contacts in the system?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can Blocked Contacts be Deleted in Business ByDesign?
entry.content:
Once the master data is created, it cannot be deleted. If you don't intend to use the master data further, you can mark it as Obsolete / Not Active / Blocked.

Master Data is the data that is used over and again without any changes for a longer period of time. It is the Core Data that is essential for operations in a specific business or business Unit. Characteristic can bear Master Data. Master Data can be Attribute, text or Hierarchy
E.g.. Accounts, Contacts, Products, Materials etc.

You can also refer to KBA: https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2185206

entry.title: SAC - Filtering Column Dimension Member Combinations to Hide Unnecessary Combinations
entry.content:
Maybe many users know how to do this but I just want to share it.

In our planning process, we need to prepare the Budget for the next year (2026) and Long Range Planning (LRP) for future years (2027, 2028, 2029, and beyond). Consequently, the column will contain a combination of Version and Date. This setup can result in unwanted combinations of Version and Year, such as Budget 2027, Budget 2028, or LRP 2026.

Of course, if there is no data for certain combinations and we turn off 'show unbooked data,' the issue is less problematic. However, if data exists for combinations like Budget 2027, it will be displayed, as shown in the above screenshot.

To resolve this issue, we need to use 'Advanced Filtering'.

If you create it as below screenshot, it will solve the issue.

If you do not want to see <all> in the date dimension, please add additional filter against date.

There is another way to do using a 'Calculated Dimension'.

Add a Calculated Dimension in the column that includes Version and Year information, and apply filters to display only the desired combinations.

Unfortunately, 'Advanced Filtering' is not supported in SAC Script yet, so if you need to change the filter dynamically for future maintaining, this solution may help. 

Steps to create the Calculated Dimension:

1. In the Columns section, click on '+ Add Dimensions,' then select '+ Create Calculated Dimension     

 

2. Enter the following formula:   [DimVersion].[p/ID] + [DimDate].[p/Year].  Put the name as Ver_FYear

3. Add this Calculated Dimension to the column dimension.

4. Now, we can hide the Ver_FYear column by reducing its height. Then, apply filters to the Calculated Dimension members and time based on the requirements.

Here is the result screenshot.

Ver_FYear column height was not reduced for explaining it, but you can hide it with reducing its height.

If anyone has a better solution, please leave any comment for all of us.

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Download PDF locally (SAP R3, 4.0B)
entry.content: You may paste directly your screenshots (ctrl+V). The rule to open a non-text file is to use BINARY (but anyway, it's flexible because one character = one byte). In debug if you read in binary to X type, you'll get something that starts with 25504446 (equals %PDF in ASCII). You save WS_DOWNLOAD with the BIN mode. Your code may contain errors at many places, so please attach it if you want a clear answer.

entry.title: UPN Change update
entry.content:
We are updating the windows user login from last name and initial to first and last name.  Our IT department is asking to Check the SAP CMS and Audit DB - can accommodate 240 characters to support UPN changes. Also want to know the impact when we switch from the current format user ID to the new format user id.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: UPN Change update
entry.content: The supported length for UPN changes in BO 4.3 is limited to 255 characters. When transitioning to the new UPN format, it is important to update all user mappings in the CMS. This includes updating user IDs in reports, schedules, and security settings, as well as updating the Single Sign-On (SSO) configuration to reflect the new UPN format. Once the change is implemented, the audit logs will start recording the new UPNs. It is necessary to update any reporting or monitoring tools that rely on these logs to handle the new format. We recommend testing the new UPN login format with a pilot group before rolling it out to all users.

entry.title: Calculo de base reguladora en caso de recaída
entry.content:
¿Cómo se calcula la base reguladora en caso de recaída por incapacidad temporal?

EL CASO:

El Tribunal Supremo se ha pronunciado sobre una cuestión relevante para la gestión de las incapacidades temporales: el cálculo de la base reguladora en casos de recaída.

El conflicto surge cuando una mutua recurre una sentencia que fijaba la base reguladora en 2.703,33 euros, coincidente con la reconocida en un proceso previo. La mutua argumentaba que debía recalcularse conforme a las cotizaciones del mes anterior a la recaída, resultando en 1.597,18 euros.

LA CUESTIÓN JURÍDICA:

El punto de debate es si, en caso de recaída, la base reguladora debe mantenerse inalterada respecto del primer proceso o recalcularse en función de las cotizaciones más recientes. Este aspecto no está expresamente regulado en el Decreto 1646/1972, lo que ha llevado al TS a interpretarlo conforme a la finalidad de la prestación.

DOCTRINA DEL TRIBUNAL SUPREMO:

1.- Finalidad de la prestación:
La IT tiene como objetivo compensar la pérdida de ingresos durante una baja laboral. Por tanto, el TS considera lógico que la base reguladora refleje la situación económica más próxima al momento de la recaída.

2.- Proporcionalidad entre cotización y prestación:
El TS recalca que la prestación debe guardar relación con las cotizaciones realizadas recientemente, evitando situaciones de ventaja económica injustificada o desajustes en el sistema.

3.- Precedentes jurisprudenciales: El TS ha mantenido este criterio en sentencias anteriores (STS 2-10-2003; STS 12-7-2007, reiterando que el cálculo de la base reguladora debe responder a las circunstancias concurrentes al momento de la recaída.

DECISIÓN DEL TS:

Estima el recurso de la mutua y fija la base reguladora en 1.597,18 euros, calculada en función de las cotizaciones más recientes. Este fallo refuerza la aplicación del principio de proporcionalidad y asegura una adecuada relación entre lo cotizado y la prestación percibida.

CONCLUSIÓN:

El Supremo deja claro que, en los casos de recaída por IT, la base reguladora debe recalcularse en atención a las cotizaciones más próximas al nuevo proceso de baja. Este criterio proporciona seguridad jurídica y garantiza la sostenibilidad del sistema de seguridad social.

(STS 11-09-2024) Link: https://vlex.es/vid/1050309809

Post original en LinkdIn : 

https://www.linkedin.com/posts/%C3%A1lvaro-robles-leal-28b278120_derecholaboral-incapacidadtemporal-seguridadsocial-activity-7274295887680425984-8bPV?utm_source=share&utm_medium=member_desktop

M.

entry.title: How to Call RAP Actions with Table Types from a List Report
entry.content:
Introduction

In this blog, we’ll explore how to define an action in the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) that accepts a table-type parameter and returns a single result. Additionally, we’ll walk through how to integrate this action into a List Report.

The motivation for exploring this approach came from a requirement to generate a PDF report based on the data from multiple selected rows. While the standard actions in a List Report allow for returning a single result using "invocationGrouping": #CHANGE_SET, we, as developers, cannot easily handle such results within custom logic. This led to the idea of creating a custom action.

For this blog, we’ll demonstrate the implementation using a simpler scenario, focusing on how to realize this functionality step by step.

Scenario Overview

We will create a simple application focused on calculating inventory values for products. In a List Report, users can select one or more products from the list and trigger the action via a button. The total value will then be displayed in a popup dialog.

Development Environment

  • Backend: BTP ABAP Environment (trial)
  • Frontend: SAP Business Application Studio

Development Steps

  • Backend

    • Table Definition
    • Defining an Action to Handle Deep Parameters
    • Implement Behavior Logic
  • Frontend
    • Creating a Fiori Elements Application
    • Implementing the Custom Action

Backend

1. Table Definition

Define a simple table containing product information. 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Product Stock'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zyasu_stock {

  key client      : abap.clnt not null;
  key id          : sysuuid_x16 not null;
  product_id      : abap.char(20);
  price           : abap.int2;
  stock           : abap.int2;
  created_by      : abp_creation_user;
  created_at      : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;
  last_changed_at : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
}

 

After creating the table, generate a RAP BO and an OData service using the "Generate ABAP Repository Objects" wizard.

2. Defining an Action to Handle Deep Parameters

An action calcStockAmountDeep has been added to the auto-generated behavior definition. This action receives a deep parameter, ZA_PRODUCT_ROOT.

 

static action calcStockAmountDeep deep parameter ZA_PRODUCT_ROOT result [1] ZA_AMOUNT;

 

Initially, I was going to use the deep table parameter because my requirement was to pass a single level table entity. However, as posted in this Q&A,  this statement does not seem to be supported yet, so I used the deep parameter instead.

To define an action parameter that takes a deep structure as input, the following objects will be created:

Root Abstract Entity

This entity contains a table of products. A "dummy" is defined because at least one property must be present in the root entity.

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Deep Parameter Root'
define root abstract entity ZA_PRODUCT_ROOT
{
    dummy: abap.char(1); 
    _product : composition [0..*] of ZA_PRODUCT_TABLE;
}

 

Child Abstract Entity

This entity contains the key fields of the product entity. An "isActiveEntity" field is added, because the service is draft-enabled. This is necessary if you want to handle both active entities and draft entities in the event handler. 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Deep Parameter for Products'
define abstract entity ZA_PRODUCT_TABLE
{
  productUuId : sysuuid_x16;
  isActiveEntity: abap_boolean;
  
  _root: association to parent ZA_PRODUCT_ROOT;
}

 

Abstract Behavior Definition

An abstract behavior definition is required to use the abstract entity as a deep parameter for an action.

 

abstract;
strict ( 2 );
with hierarchy;
define behavior for ZA_PRODUCT_ROOT alias Root
{
    association _product;
}

define behavior for ZA_PRODUCT_TABLE alias Product
{
}

 

3. Implement Behavior Logic

First, define the following type in the private section of the behavior handler class. This type will be used to extract the product keys from the parameters passed to the action. 

 

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_key,
             id       TYPE sysuuid_x16,
             is_draft TYPE abp_behv_flag,
           END OF ty_key.

 

Next, implement the calcStockAmountDeep method as follows.

 

  METHOD calcStockAmountDeep.
    DATA amount TYPE i.
    DATA lt_productuuid TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_key.

    "get product keys
    LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(key).
      LOOP AT key-%param-_product INTO DATA(product).
        APPEND VALUE #( id = product-productUuId
                        is_draft = SWITCH #( product-isActiveEntity
                                              WHEN abap_true THEN if_abap_behv=>mk-off
                                                             ELSE if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) TO lt_productuuid.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.

    READ ENTITIES OF zr_yasu_stock IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY Stock
      FIELDS ( Price Stock )
      WITH VALUE #( FOR data IN lt_productuuid (
          %tky = VALUE #( id = data-id
                          %is_draft = data-is_draft ) ) )
      RESULT DATA(stock_t).

    " calculate stock amount
    LOOP AT stock_t INTO DATA(stock).
      amount = amount + stock-Price * stock-Stock.
    ENDLOOP.

    " return result
    result = VALUE #( FOR key1 IN keys (
                       %cid = key1-%cid
                       %param =  VALUE #( amount = amount )
                     ) ).

  ENDMETHOD.

 

With the backend implementation complete, we will test the action by calling it from Postman. Before invoking the action, use a GET method to retrieve the CSRF token, and include it in the headers when making the action call. You can confirm that the total stock value is returned successfully.

Frontend

1. Creating a Fiori Elements Application

In SAP Business Application Studio, select the OData service created in the previous step and use the List Report template to create the application. Since we want to implement the logic using TypeScript, select the "Enable TypeScript" option in the "Project Attributes" during the project setup process.

2. Implementing the Custom Action

From the Page Map, add an action to the table's toolbar by selecting "Add Custom Action".

In the "New Custom Action" dialog, define the action as follows. (Although not visible in the screenshot, the Handler Method was set to getTotalDeep.)

Implement the action in ext/controller/GetTotalDeep.ts as follows.

 

import ExtensionAPI from 'sap/fe/core/ExtensionAPI';
import Context from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v4/Context';
import ListReportExtensionAPI from 'sap/fe/templates/ListReport/ExtensionAPI';
import ODataContextBinding from 'sap/ui/model/odata/v4/ODataContextBinding';
import MessageBox from 'sap/m/MessageBox';
import NumberFormat from 'sap/ui/core/format/NumberFormat';

interface Error {
	message: string
	error: { 
		code: string
		details: Error[] 
	}
}

interface Result {
    amount: string
}

const namespace = "com.sap.gateway.srvd.zui_yasu_stock_o4.v0001."

/**
 * Generated event handler.
 *
 *  this reference to the 'this' that the event handler is bound to.
 *  pageContext the context of the page on which the event was fired
 */
export function getTotalDeep(this: ExtensionAPI, pageContext: Context) {
    //get selected contexts
    const ListReportExtensionAPI = this as ListReportExtensionAPI;
    const contexts = ListReportExtensionAPI.getSelectedContexts();
    const products = contexts.map(context => {
        return {
            productUuId: context.getProperty("Id"),
            isActiveEntity: context.getProperty("IsActiveEntity")
        }
    })

    //invoke the action
    const model = ListReportExtensionAPI.getModel();
    const operation = model?.bindContext("/Stock/" + namespace + "calcStockAmountDeep(...)") as ODataContextBinding;
    operation.setParameter("dummy", "X");
    operation.setParameter("_product", products);
    
    const fnSuccess = () => {
        //show total
        const result = operation.getBoundContext().getObject() as Result;
        const amount = result.amount;
        const numberFormat = NumberFormat.getIntegerInstance({
            groupingEnabled: true
        });
        var formattedAmount = numberFormat.format(amount);
        MessageBox.show(`${formattedAmount} JPY`, {
            title: "Stock Amount"
        });
    }

    const fnError = (error:Error) => {
        MessageBox.error(error.message);
    }

    operation.invoke().then(fnSuccess, fnError);

}

 

Conclusion

In this blog, we explored how to implement a custom action in RAP that processes deep parameters and integrate it with a Fiori Elements List Report. We demonstrated the end-to-end process, from backend implementation to frontend integration.

Note that in a List Report, clicking "Select All" only selects the rows currently visible on the screen (refer to SAP Note 2428401). As a result, the approach introduced here is best suited for use cases where a few rows from the displayed data are selected, rather than scenarios involving bulk selection of large datasets.
